core_contribution: A maximum-likelihood approach that jointly learns a classifier, annotator reliability parameters (sensitivity/specificity), and estimates true labels from multiple noisy annotators without gold standard. Uses EM algorithm with a two-coin noise model for each annotator.
algorithm_steps: E-step: Estimate posterior probability of true labels given current classifier and annotator parameters
M-step: Update classifier weights w using weighted logistic regression with estimated true labels
M-step: Update annotator sensitivity αj and specificity βj parameters based on estimated true labels
Iterate E and M steps until convergence
key_equations: Eq 1-2: Sensitivity αj = Pr[yj=1|y=1] and specificity βj = Pr[yj=0|y=0] definitions
Eq 3: Logistic regression model Pr[y=1|x,w] = σ(w⊤x)
Section 2.5: E-step posterior computation for true labels
Section 2.5: M-step updates for classifier and annotator parameters

key_assumptions.assumption: Annotator sensitivity and specificity are instance-independent (αj and βj constant across all instances)
key_assumptions.why_needed: Enables parameter estimation with limited data per annotator
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When annotator performance varies significantly across different types of instances or subgroups

key_assumptions.assumption: Annotator labels are conditionally independent given the true label
key_assumptions.why_needed: Simplifies likelihood factorization and parameter estimation
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When annotators influence each other or have correlated biases

key_assumptions.assumption: True labels follow the logistic regression model
key_assumptions.why_needed: Provides parametric form for classifier learning
key_assumptions.violation_scenario: When decision boundary is highly non-linear or data is not linearly separable

baselines_compared: Treating each annotation as separate example
baseline_limitations_identified: Majority voting assumes all annotators are equally reliable and can be dominated by poor but numerous annotators
proposed_improvement: Automatically discovers and weights annotators by their estimated reliability (sensitivity/specificity) while jointly learning the classifier
condition_where_baseline_wins: When all annotators have similar and high reliability, majority voting might be simpler and equally effective
suitable_toy_datasets: Synthetic binary classification with multiple simulated annotators
MNIST with artificially added annotator noise
Iris dataset with simulated expert disagreement
toy_dataset_justification: These allow controlled simulation of annotator noise patterns and validation against known ground truth
reproducibility_notes: EM algorithm implementation is straightforward but requires careful initialization and convergence criteria. Annotator noise simulation details not fully specified.

key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Simultaneously estimates classifier and annotator parameters
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Sequential approach (first estimate labels, then learn classifier) would likely perform worse

key_components_for_ablation.role_in_method: Incorporates prior knowledge about annotator reliability
key_components_for_ablation.expected_effect_of_removal: Would reduce to pure maximum likelihood without regularization
Instance-specific parameters would be unidentifiable without more data

known_failure_modes.scenario: When annotator performance varies significantly across instance types
known_failure_modes.reason: Violates the instance-independence assumption for annotator parameters
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: Annotator sensitivity and specificity are instance-independent

known_failure_modes.scenario: With very few annotations per annotator
known_failure_modes.reason: Insufficient data to reliably estimate annotator-specific parameters
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: Sufficient data available to estimate per-annotator sensitivity/specificity

known_failure_modes.scenario: When annotators have systematic correlated biases
known_failure_modes.related_assumption: Annotator labels are conditionally independent given the true label
